reasoning: The film 'Springsteen: Deliver Me from Nowhere' is a biopic about Bruce Springsteen recording his stripped-down acoustic album 'Nebraska' in a rented house in New Jersey in 1982. Historically, the Academy Award for Best Production Design almost exclusively goes to films with lavish period settings, massive sci-fi/fantasy world-building, or highly stylized visuals (e.g., Poor Things, Dune, Mad Max: Fury Road, The Grand Budapest Hotel). An intimate, gritty drama set in early-1980s New Jersey is extremely unlikely to feature the kind of showy production design that the Academy typically recognizes in this category. The base rate for any given prestige film winning this specific award is already low (around 1-3%), and the subject matter of this film makes its chances even lower. Without evidence of an uncharacteristically elaborate visual design approach, the probability of it winning Best Production Design is at the minimum threshold (2%).
